description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the alpine Docker image with four stages: compile, test, package, and ssh-stage. Add a compile job in the compile stage that runs `cat file1.txt file2.txt > compiled.txt` and stores `compiled.txt` as an artifact for 20 minutes. Add a test job in the test stage that runs `cat compiled.txt | grep -q 'Hello world'`. In the package stage add a pack-gz job that runs `gzip -k compiled.txt` then `mv compiled.txt.gz package.gz` and publishes `package.gz` as a 20‑minute artifact, and a pack-iso job that first executes `echo "ipv6" >> /etc/modules`, `apk update`, `apk add xorriso` then runs `mkisofs -o ./packaged.iso ./compiled.txt` and stores `packaged.iso` as a 20‑minute artifact. Finally add an update-repo job in the ssh-stage that runs a series of before‑script commands (`echo "ipv6" >> /etc/modules`, `apk update`, `apk add openssh-client`, `apk add bash bash-doc bash-completion`, `apk add git`, `eval $(ssh-agent -s)`, `bash -c "ssh-add <(echo '$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY')"`, `mkdir -p ~/.ssh`, conditional SSH config, `ssh git@gitlab.com`) and a script that creates a date.txt file, checks out a new master branch, rewrites the remote to git@gitlab.com:kubouch/ci-test.git, configures Git user, commits and pushes the date file; this job runs only on scheduled pipelines or manual web triggers.
